summary: dotenvv-tool postinstall exfiltrates host and filesystem data
details: The package's postinstall.js enumerates the filesystem (fs.existsSync checks across multiple paths beginning around line 134), reads host and user identity via os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir(), and issues outbound HTTP traffic via the node http module. The package name is also a likely confusion attack against the widely-used dotenv package. Running `npm install dotenvv-tool` causes collection of installer host/user/filesystem metadata and transmission off-host at install time, without consent and without any relation to a legitimate advertised function. This is an install-time credential/recon beacon pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
